Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE1: Search emails using search_emails (query: "Deshawn Hartley") to find Deshawn's formal complaint email (email_deshawn_julian_complaint_apr25, timestamp 1777111200, INBOX) reporting that his apartment and belongings were not ready when he arrived in Denver on April 20 for the Automate 2026 trade show, that he stayed at the Hyatt Regency for five nights at $1,240 out of pocket, that belongings arrived April 23, and that Tamara Byrd is aware. Read/lookup action.
OE2: Search emails in the SENT folder using search_emails (folder_name: "SENT", query: "Deshawn") to find Julian Brooks's prior promise email (email_julian_deshawn_promise_apr17, timestamp 1776434400) sent April 17, in which Julian told Deshawn his apartment would be ready and belongings delivered before the 21st, named Suki Patel as coordinator, and said Deshawn would hear from Julian directly if anything changed. This establishes what Julian committed to and what the response email must account for. Read/lookup action.
OE3: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Deshawn Hartley") to find Suki Patel's message in #operations (C006, ts 1776697200.000000) noting a storage facility scheduling conflict causing "a few days of slippage," that Julian said he would handle communication with Deshawn, and pointing to the tracking ticket for details. Also find Emeka Diallo's message in #customer-engagement (C002, ts 1776942000.000000) from April 23 saying Deshawn's stuff was delivered and "he hasn't raised anything so I think we're in the clear." Read/lookup action.
OE4: Search CRM using crm_search_companies (name: "Mosaic") to find company_mosaic, then crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_mosaic") to find deal_mosaic_hartley_apr2026 ($1,520, inprogress, hard April 19 deadline), and crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_mosaic"]) to find engagement_mosaic_tamara_deshawn_apr22 documenting Tamara Byrd's April 22 call to Emeka about Deshawn's status, her concern about the $45,000 Automate booth investment, VP of Engineering Rafael Mendes watching closely, and Emeka's admission that he did not follow up with Suki or get back to Tamara. Read/lookup action.
OE5: Search Linear using linear_list_issues (query: "Deshawn" or "Mosaic") to find issue_mosaic_hartley_relocation (number 399, state in_progress, assigned to moveops_suki_patel), whose description shows the Tampa to Denver route, April 14-19 move window, Automate 2026 hard deadline, Swift Relocations as vendor, Suki Patel as coordinator, and status updates through April 14 pickup. The description does NOT mention the delay or any problems. Read/lookup action.
OE6: Call linear_list_comments on issue_mosaic_hartley_relocation to find comment_suki_hartley_delay_apr20 (created April 20). THIS IS THE CRITICAL STEP. The comment contains facts that exist nowhere else: storage intake rescheduled from April 18 to April 22 due to a loading dock scheduling conflict flagged April 17, apartment key handoff from UrbanNest pushed to April 23 because the landlord delayed unit turnover by 3 days (a separate issue from the storage conflict), $385 rush delivery surcharge charged by Swift for an attempted timeline compression, and Suki's statement that she told Julian about the slip on April 18 and he said he would handle client communication. No agent in any observed run called linear_list_comments. Read/lookup action.
OE7: Search Airtable using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "Mosaic") to find all Mosaic relocation records including recDeshawnHartleyTampaDenver (Status: In Progress, Origin: Tampa FL, Destination: Denver CO, Move Start Date: 2026-04-14, Move End Date: 2026-04-19, Assigned Coordinator: Suki Patel). Also finds other Mosaic relocations for Zara Okoye, Keiko Tanaka, Lena Kowalski, Andre Mitchell, Andre Washington (Completed), and Tanya Reeves (Completed) to build the broader account picture. Read/lookup action.
OE8: Search QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria matching Mosaic or DocNumber "INV-2026-0411") to find the $9,010 invoice covering 3 Mosaic April relocations including Deshawn's $1,520 standard package. Read/lookup action.
OE9: Search QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_search_bills for Swift Relocations vendor to find bill_swift_hartley_rush_apr2026 (DocNumber SW-RUSH-DH-0420, TotalAmt $385, TxnDate 2026-04-20), the rush delivery surcharge for Deshawn's relocation that was charged by Swift but never communicated to the client. This is the only record of the $385 cost. No agent in any observed run searched QuickBooks bills. Read/lookup action.
OE10: Investigate broader Mosaic account issues by searching emails (query: "Mosaic"), Slack (search_query: "Mosaic"), and Linear (query: "Mosaic") to discover the pattern of service failures: Zara Okoye prototype damage ($90K, $40K insurance gap), Keiko Tanaka missing DOT placarding certificate, Andre Washington duplicate receipt flag, and ExpenseBot false approvals from malformed JSON config. Read/lookup action.
OE11: Look up contact information using contacts_search_contacts for Emeka Diallo, Suki Patel, and Julian Brooks to get email addresses for write actions. Deshawn Hartley's email (deshawn.hartley@mosaicrobotics.com) is already available from the complaint email sender field in OE1, or can be found via crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Deshawn Hartley") since he is not in the Contacts service but exists in CRM contacts. Read/lookup action.
OE12: Reply to Deshawn's complaint email using reply_to_email or send_email (sender: julian.brooks@moveops.com, recipient: deshawn.hartley@mosaicrobotics.com) with a full explanation of what went wrong, ownership of the communication failure, commitment to reimburse the $1,240 hotel cost, and statement that Emeka is being looped in. Write action.
OE13: Send email to Emeka Diallo using send_email (sender: julian.brooks@moveops.com, recipient: emeka.diallo@moveops.com) briefing him on the Deshawn escalation, the broader Mosaic account risk pattern, the need to contact Tamara Byrd, and requesting alignment on a recovery plan. Write action.
OE14: Create a Linear escalation issue using linear_create_issue (team: Operations or Team Relocations) to track the Deshawn Hartley incident and remediation, including the timeline, root cause analysis, financial impact, and action items. Write action.
OE15: Post a message in #customer-support using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C007) alerting the team to the live Mosaic escalation with context on the broader account risk. Write action.
OE16: Update Deshawn's Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01, record: recDeshawnHartleyTampaDenver) to change Status from "In Progress" to "Completed" and add incident details to Special Requirements. Write action.
OE17: Update the original Linear ticket using linear_update_issue (id: issue_mosaic_hartley_relocation) to mark it as Done and add the full delay timeline to the description. Write action.
OE18: Schedule a meeting with Emeka using calendar_add_calendar_event with attendees including julian.brooks@moveops.com and emeka.diallo@moveops.com, before end of week (by May 1, 2026). Write action.
OE19: Update the stale CRM deal for Deshawn's relocation using crm_update_deal_stage (id: "deal_mosaic_hartley_apr2026", dealstage: "closedwon" or similar) to reflect the completed move. The deal currently shows "inprogress" but the relocation was delivered April 23. Write action.
OE20: Create a CRM engagement note using crm_create_engagement (company_ids: ["company_mosaic"]) documenting the escalation, findings, actions taken, and broader account risk assessment. Write action (implicit, not explicitly stated in prompt but clearly needed).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
